# Copilot Instructions

<!-- This is NOT the Next.js you know. This version has breaking changes — APIs,
conventions, and file structure may differ from training data. Read the relevant
guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. -->

## Stack

- **Next.js 16.2.7** (App Router, Turbopack) — breaking changes vs. earlier versions
- **Tailwind CSS v4** — no `tailwind.config.js`; configuration lives in `app/globals.css` via `@theme inline` and `@custom-variant`
- **TypeScript** (strict mode)

## Commands

```bash
npm run dev       # dev server (Turbopack)
npm run build     # production build
npm run start     # start production server
npx tsc --noEmit  # type-check without building
node_modules/.bin/next typegen .  # regenerate PageProps/LayoutProps types (run after adding routes)
```

> `npm run lint` currently fails due to a missing `n` plugin in `eslint.config.mjs` — this is a pre-existing issue, not a blocker.

## Architecture

This is a **statically pre-rendered, single-page portfolio** with i18n routing.

### Routing & i18n

- `proxy.ts` (root, not `middleware.ts` — renamed in Next.js 16) redirects `/` and locale-less paths to `/es`.
- All pages live under `app/[lang]/`. Supported locales: `es` (default), `en`, `ca`.
- `generateStaticParams()` in `app/[lang]/layout.tsx` pre-renders `/es`, `/en`, `/ca` at build time.
- Locale validation (`isLocale`) + `notFound()` guard in both layout and page.

### Data / Translations

- `dictionaries/{es,en,ca}.json` hold all UI copy and portfolio content (jobs, tech categories, bio).
- `dictionaries/index.ts` exports `getDictionary(locale)`, `isLocale()`, `Locale` type, and `Dictionary` type (derived from `es.json` as canonical shape).
- **`es.json` is the canonical shape.** When adding new keys, add to all three JSON files.

### Server → Client boundary

```
app/[lang]/page.tsx          ← Server Component
  ├─ loads dict via getDictionary()
  ├─ renders HomeSection, ExperienceSection, TechnologySection (Server Components)
  └─ passes them + dict + lang to <PortfolioPage> (Client Component)

app/[lang]/components/PortfolioPage.tsx   ← 'use client'
  ├─ owns activeSection state (persisted in URL hash)
  └─ renders <Navbar> + shows/hides the three pre-rendered section nodes
```

Section nodes are **server-rendered React nodes passed as children** to the client wrapper — they don't re-render on section switch, only visibility changes via `style.display`.

### Theme

- **Dark is the default** (`:root` CSS vars) — browser `prefers-color-scheme` is intentionally ignored.
- Light mode is activated by adding class `light` to `<html>` (`html.light { ... }` overrides in `globals.css`).
- An inline `<script>` in `app/[lang]/layout.tsx`'s `<head>` reads `localStorage('theme')` before first paint and adds `light` class if needed. `<html>` has `suppressHydrationWarning`.
- Theme toggle (in `Navbar.tsx`) adds/removes `light` class and writes `localStorage.setItem('theme', 'dark'|'light')`.

### Tailwind v4 color tokens

Colors are CSS variables bridged to Tailwind utilities via `@theme inline` in `globals.css`:

| CSS var | Tailwind class | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `--surface` | `bg-surface` | Page background |
| `--card` | `bg-card` | Card/panel background |
| `--fg` | `text-fg` | Primary text |
| `--muted` | `text-muted` | Secondary text |
| `--accent` | `text-accent` / `bg-accent` | Brand color (indigo) |
| `--accent-dim` | `bg-accent-dim` | Subtle accent tint |
| `--border-color` | `border-border` | All borders |

To add a new color: define it in `:root` and `html.light` in `globals.css`, then add a `--color-*: var(--*)` entry under `@theme inline`.

## Key Conventions

### Next.js 16 specifics

- Middleware file is `proxy.ts` (not `middleware.ts` — deprecated and renamed).
- `params` is a **Promise** in Next.js 16 — always `await props.params`.
- Use globally available `PageProps<'/[lang]'>` and `LayoutProps<'/[lang]'>` helpers (generated by `next typegen`); no import needed.
- No `app/layout.tsx` — `app/[lang]/layout.tsx` is the root layout (owns `<html>` and `<body>`).

### ESLint rules (enforced)

- Single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space indent
- No trailing commas
- `prefer-const` (destructuring: `'all'`)

### Adding a new section

1. Create `app/[lang]/components/XSection.tsx` (Server Component).
2. Add translation keys to all three JSON files (keep `es.json` as reference shape).
3. Import and render in `app/[lang]/page.tsx`, pass as child to `<PortfolioPage>`.
4. Add the section id to `SECTIONS` in `PortfolioPage.tsx` and add the nav label key to `nav` in the JSON files.
